message:
chore(beads): update issue tracking with task status and metadata changes

Sync beads issue tracker with current state of migration tasks (T3.2
through T3.5) and related installation work items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -613,11 +613,11 @@
 {"id":"br-28mgh.3.2","title":"T3.2: Implement timestamp conversion functions","description":"Implement the core timestamp conversion logic for Python TEXT to Rust i64.

Python format examples:
- "2026-02-24 15:30:00.123456" (most common: space separator, microseconds)
- "2026-02-24T15:30:00.123456" (ISO-8601 with T separator)
- "2026-02-24 15:30:00" (no fractional seconds)
- "2026-02-24" (date only, seen in some expires_at fields)
- NULL (preserve as NULL)

Conversion: Parse with chrono::NaiveDateTime, convert to i64 microseconds.
Use div_euclid/rem_euclid for pre-1970 safety (already implemented in timestamps.rs).

Functions needed:
- text_to_micros(text: &str) -> Result<i64, MigrationError>
- convert_column(conn, table, column) -> Result<u64, MigrationError> (returns rows converted)

Edge cases to handle:
- Empty string -> treat as NULL
- Malformed timestamp -> log warning, skip row, continue
- Timestamp with timezone suffix -> strip and treat as UTC
- Pre-1970 timestamps -> supported via div_euclid

File: crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/migrate.rs
Depends on: T3.1 (reuses detection)

Tests: At least 10 conversion vectors covering all format variations","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:24.728487743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T06:57:15.194633085Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T06:57:15.194613789Z","close_reason":"Implemented text_to_micros(), convert_column(), convert_all_timestamps() in mcp-agent-mail-db/src/migrate.rs. Handles space/T separator, microseconds/milliseconds, date-only, timezone stripping, pre-epoch, empty strings. 21 tests total for conversion (14 parse + 4 column + 2 full migration + 1 idempotent). 0 clippy warnings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:24.728487743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:15.671864147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.3.3","title":"T3.3: Implement full database migration command","description":"Implement `am migrate` CLI command that performs the full database migration.

Flow:
1. Detect database format (T3.1)
2. If already migrated, print "Database is already in Rust format" and exit 0
3. If empty, print "Database is empty, no migration needed" and exit 0
4. Create backup: cp storage.sqlite3 storage.sqlite3.bak.TIMESTAMP
5. For each table with timestamp columns:
   a. ALTER TABLE to add _new column (INTEGER)
   b. UPDATE table SET col_new = convert(col) WHERE col IS NOT NULL
   c. Drop old column, rename new column (SQLite requires table rebuild)
6. Rebuild FTS5 virtual tables (drop and recreate with triggers)
7. VACUUM to reclaim space
8. Verify: run T3.1 detection again, confirm "AlreadyMigrated"
9. Print summary: tables migrated, rows converted, time taken, backup path

Tables and their timestamp columns:
- projects: created_at
- agents: created_at  
- messages: created_at, updated_at
- message_recipients: read_at, ack_ts
- file_reservations: created_at, expires_at, released_at
- agent_links: created_at, updated_at, expires_at
- message_summaries: created_at
- product_project_links: created_at

CLI interface:
  am migrate              # Auto-detect and migrate
  am migrate --check      # Just check, do not modify
  am migrate --force      # Migrate even if format unclear
  am migrate --backup-dir /path  # Custom backup location

File: crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs (add migrate subcommand)
File: crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/migrate.rs (migration logic)
Depends on: T3.1, T3.2","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:25.024475709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:22:47.754281635Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:22:47.754208668Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.3","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:25.024475709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.3","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:15.984302027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.3","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:16.308000379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.3","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:09:37.559911267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":906,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW FIX: The ALTER/DROP COLUMN approach only works on SQLite 3.35.0+ (2021-03). For maximum portability, use the 12-step table rebuild method: (1) BEGIN, (2) CREATE TABLE new_table with i64 columns, (3) INSERT INTO new_table SELECT with conversion, (4) DROP TABLE old_table, (5) ALTER TABLE new_table RENAME TO table, (6) recreate indices, (7) recreate triggers, (8) COMMIT. This is the standard SQLite pattern and works on all versions.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:09:26Z"},{"id":928,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: Sequencing concern with install.sh integration. The installer must: (1) Install Rust binaries first, (2) THEN call 'am migrate --check' to detect format, (3) THEN call 'am migrate' if needed. This means T3.3 cannot run during install until the binary is placed. The T2.2 integration task must sequence: install binary -> migrate DB -> update MCP configs -> verify. This is already implied but make it explicit in the install flow pseudocode.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:14:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.3.4","title":"T3.4: Migration safety and rollback","description":"Ensure migration is safe, idempotent, and rollback-capable:

1. Idempotency: Running `am migrate` twice must be safe
   - Second run detects AlreadyMigrated and exits cleanly
   - No partial migration state possible (use transactions)

2. Transaction safety:
   - Wrap each table migration in a transaction
   - If any table fails, rollback that table and continue to next
   - At end, report which tables succeeded and which failed

3. Rollback:
   - `am migrate --rollback` restores from backup
   - Lists available backups with timestamps
   - Prompts for confirmation before restoring

4. Integrity verification:
   - After migration, count rows in each table (must match pre-migration)
   - Spot-check 5 random rows per table: verify timestamp is reasonable
     (between 2020-01-01 and 2030-01-01 in microseconds)
   - Verify FTS5 search still works (run a test query)

File: crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/migrate.rs
Depends on: T3.3","notes":"2026-02-27 PurpleCliff progress: Implemented migration hardening in db/cli paths: (1) detect_timestamp_format now evaluates all timestamp columns (catches mixed format within a single table), (2) convert_all_timestamps now enforces table-level BEGIN/COMMIT transactions with rollback on table failure and migration_state tracking (including stale-state recovery), (3) CLI migrate flow now supports --rollback with backup discovery, confirmation gate, restore path, and pre-rollback backup, (4) added integrity verification pass (row-count invariants + sampled timestamp plausibility warnings), (5) added regression tests for mixed-within-table detection and stale migration-state reconversion, plus CLI parsing/backup/restore tests. Validation: rch cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-db --all-targets (pass), rch cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-db migrate -- --nocapture (35 migrate tests pass). Remaining blocker: rch cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --all-targets fails due unrelated existing Rust-2024 keyword parse errors in mcp-agent-mail-server (gen identifier in tui_bridge/tui_screens), outside this bead scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleCliff","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:25.323472819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:52:44.171708633Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T06:52:44.171682434Z","close_reason":"Completed migration safety+rollback hardening; verified via rch db/cli targeted tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.4","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:25.323472819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.4","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:16.614878109Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":913,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: Transaction-per-table is good but consider: if 3 of 8 tables migrate successfully and the 4th fails, we have a mixed-format database. The code MUST track which tables have been migrated (store a migration_state table or use a journal file). On re-run, skip already-migrated tables. The detection in T3.1 should check ALL tables, not just the first one with data.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:11:09Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-28mgh.4","title":"T4: MCP Config Takeover","description":"Track 4: MCP Config Takeover

The Python installer configures MCP client tools (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, etc.)
to point to the Python server. The Rust installer must update these configs to point to the
Rust binary instead, while preserving other MCP server configurations.

## Scope
- Detect existing MCP configurations for each agent tool
- Update server command from Python to Rust binary path
- Preserve bearer tokens and other auth settings
- Preserve non-agent-mail MCP server configs
- Handle Claude Code: ~/.config/claude/claude_desktop_config.json (macOS)
  and equivalent Windows/Linux paths
- Handle Codex CLI config
- Handle Gemini CLI config  
- Handle GitHub Copilot config
- Backup configs before modification
- Verify configs are valid JSON after modification

## Key Constraint
ONLY modify the mcp-agent-mail server entry. Leave all other MCP servers untouched.
The Python entry typically looks like:
  {"command": "python", "args": ["-m", "mcp_agent_mail", ...]}
Replace with:
  {"command": "mcp-agent-mail", "args": [...]}","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:31.668438480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:31.668438480Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["track"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:31.668438480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-28mgh.4.1","title":"T4.1: Detect MCP config file locations per agent tool","description":"Map all known MCP configuration file locations for each supported agent tool.

Agent tools and their config paths:

Claude Code (claude_desktop_config.json):
- macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json
- Linux: ~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json  
- Windows: %APPDATA%/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json

Codex CLI:
- ~/.codex/config.json or similar (check Python integrate script)

Gemini CLI:  
- Check Python integrate_gemini.sh for config location

GitHub Copilot:
- Check Python integrate script

Windsurf/Cursor/other:
- Check Python integration scripts in scripts/ directory

Implementation: Return a list of (agent_name, config_path, exists: bool) tuples.
Check the Python project scripts/integrate_*.sh for authoritative paths.

File: crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/mcp_config.rs (NEW module)
Also: install.sh shell function detect_mcp_configs()

Reference: /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail/scripts/integrate_claude_code.sh
Reference: /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail/scripts/install.sh (integration section)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleOriole","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:52.391824033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T05:14:25.250786817Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T05:14:25.250763744Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4.1","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:52.391824033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":903,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Picked up via bv --robot-next. Starting implementation: add core MCP config location detector module (crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/mcp_config.rs), export via core lib, and add install.sh detect_mcp_configs() shell helper aligned to known agent config paths from setup.rs + legacy integration scripts.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:05:05Z"},{"id":926,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented: added core MCP config path detector module at crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/mcp_config.rs with structured output (tool/path/exists), home+project+APPDATA path catalogs, deterministic dedupe, and unit tests for required paths/existence/dedup. Exported module/types/functions via crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/lib.rs. Added install.sh helper detect_mcp_configs() and post-install reporting of detected config files (with AM_INSTALL_LIST_ALL_MCP_CONFIGS=1 opt-in for full path listing). Validation: bash -n install.sh; cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-core mcp_config -- --nocapture; cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:13:45Z"},{"id":927,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: The Codex, Gemini, and Copilot config paths are underspecified. Check these actual known paths: Codex CLI uses ~/.codex/config.json (or via CODEX_CONFIG env var). Cursor uses ~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/globalStorage/saoudrizwan.claude-dev/settings/cline_mcp_settings.json on macOS. Windsurf uses ~/Library/Application Support/Windsurf/User/globalStorage/... similar pattern. Also check the existing setup.rs in the project - it already has path definitions for many of these tools.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:14:03Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-28mgh.4.2","title":"T4.2: Parse and update MCP config JSON","description":"Parse MCP config files (JSON) and update the mcp-agent-mail server entry.

The typical Python entry looks like:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "mcp-agent-mail": {
      "command": "python",
      "args": ["-m", "mcp_agent_mail", "serve-stdio"],
      "env": {"STORAGE_ROOT": "...", "HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN": "..."}
    }
  }
}

OR (with uvx):
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "mcp-agent-mail": {
      "command": "/path/to/venv/bin/python",
      "args": ["-m", "mcp_agent_mail"],
      "env": {...}
    }
  }
}

Replace with:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "mcp-agent-mail": {
      "command": "/Users/jeff/.local/bin/mcp-agent-mail",
      "args": [],
      "env": {"STORAGE_ROOT": "...", "HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN": "..."}
    }
  }
}

Rules:
1. ONLY modify the "mcp-agent-mail" entry (match by key name)
2. Preserve ALL other mcpServers entries untouched  
3. Preserve env vars (especially HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN, STORAGE_ROOT)
4. Use absolute path to Rust binary
5. If Python entry has custom args, translate to Rust equivalents
6. Backup config before modification
7. Validate JSON is still valid after modification

File: crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/mcp_config.rs
Also: install.sh function update_mcp_configs()
Depends on: T4.1","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:52.710345223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:32:08.237891584Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:52.710345223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:16.926528434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":919,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: When modifying MCP config JSON, preserve the original formatting style (indentation, key order). Use serde_json with preserve_order feature. Also handle: (1) JSON with comments (some MCP configs use JSONC), (2) Trailing commas (common user edit), (3) UTF-8 BOM (Windows). If the file has JSONC features, consider using a JSONC-aware parser or stripping comments before parse and reinserting after.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:11:38Z"},{"id":924,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: Important edge case: The Python MCP entry often uses 'command': 'python' (relying on PATH/venv). The Rust replacement MUST use the absolute path to the binary, not just 'mcp-agent-mail', because MCP agent tools invoke the command directly (not through a shell that resolves PATH). Use the actual installed path, e.g., /Users/jeff/.local/bin/mcp-agent-mail. The installer knows this path -- pass it to the config update function.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:13:34Z"},{"id":929,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting implementation: add robust MCP config JSON updater in core mcp_config.rs (only mutate mcp-agent-mail entry, preserve env + sibling servers, handle BOM/comment/trailing-comma tolerant parse) and wire install.sh update_mcp_configs() with backup + validation.","created_at":"2026-02-25T06:39:47Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-28mgh.4.3","title":"T4.3: Create fresh MCP configs for new installs","description":"For fresh installs (no Python), offer to set up MCP configs for detected agent tools.

Flow:
1. Detect which agent tools are installed (claude, codex, gemini, copilot, cursor, windsurf)
   - Check: command -v claude, command -v codex, etc.
   - Check: presence of config directories
2. For each detected tool:
   a. Check if MCP config file exists
   b. If yes, check if mcp-agent-mail entry already exists
   c. If no entry, offer to add one (with default settings)
3. Generate bearer token if not provided
4. Write config with proper JSON formatting (preserve_order, pretty print)

Interactive mode: prompt for each tool
Non-interactive: auto-configure all detected tools

File: install.sh (function setup_mcp_configs())
File: crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/mcp_config.rs (Rust equivalent for am setup-mcp)

-{"id":"br-28mgh.4.4","title":"T4.4: Bearer token migration and generation","description":"Handle bearer token continuity between Python and Rust installations.

The Python installer generates a bearer token and stores it in:
1. The .env file (HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN=...)
2. The MCP config files (env.HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN)
3. scripts/run_server_with_token.sh

Migration:
1. Extract token from any of these sources (priority: MCP config > .env > script)
2. Verify token is a valid hex string
3. Pass token to Rust installation (write to .env or pass via env var)
4. If no token found, generate a new one (32 bytes hex)
5. Ensure all MCP configs use the same token

File: install.sh (function migrate_bearer_token())
Depends on: T4.2, T1.3 (needs Python clone path to find .env)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:53.320589902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T05:11:42.216108786Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4.4","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:18.785589734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4.4","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:53.320589902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4.4","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:21:18.489859994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":920,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.4.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: Bearer token continuity is critical. If the token changes during migration, all connected agent tools will lose authentication until they restart. Priority order for token sources should be: (1) MCP config (authoritative, what agent tools are currently using), (2) .env file, (3) run_server_with_token.sh. If tokens differ across sources, use the MCP config token (since that is what active agent sessions are using) and update the .env to match.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:11:42Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-28mgh.4.5","title":"T4.5: am setup-mcp CLI command","description":"Add am setup-mcp CLI command for configuring MCP agent tool integrations post-install. Useful for: adding newly installed agent tools, repairing broken MCP configs, updating after manual changes. CLI: am setup-mcp (auto-detect all), --tool claude (specific tool), --list (status), --dry-run (preview). For each detected tool: detect config via T4.1 module, check if entry exists, offer to add/update using T4.2 logic, generate/reuse bearer token via T4.4. Interactive mode: prompt per tool. Non-interactive (--yes): auto-configure all. File: crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs (add setup-mcp subcommand).  {"id":"br-28mgh.5","title":"T5: Storage and Config Migration","description":"Track 5: Storage and Config Migration

Both Python and Rust versions use the same storage root (~/.mcp_agent_mail_git_mailbox_repo)
and the same Git-backed archive format. Config uses .env files. This track ensures seamless
continuity of storage and configuration.

## Scope
- Verify Git archive compatibility (Python and Rust use same format)
- Migrate .env file settings if present
- Migrate notification signals directory (~/.mcp_agent_mail/signals)
- Handle storage_root customization (env var or config)
- Detect and handle database location differences (./storage.sqlite3 vs absolute path)
- Create config directory if needed (~/.config/mcp-agent-mail/)

## Key Insight
The Git archive format is already identical between Python and Rust. No conversion needed.
The .env format is also compatible. The main risk is database path resolution -- Python
uses a relative path by default while Rust resolves it relative to storage_root.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:32.023284337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:32.023284337Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["track"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.5","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:32.023284337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.5.1","title":"T5.1: Verify Git archive compatibility","description":"Verify that the Python Git archive at ~/.mcp_agent_mail_git_mailbox_repo is
fully compatible with the Rust version.

Both versions use the same Git-based archive format:
- Per-project directories under the storage root
- metadata.json files
- Message bundles in YYYY/MM/DD/ subdirectories
- Commit messages follow same convention

Verification steps:
1. Open the Git repo with git2 crate
2. Walk all commits, verify they parse correctly
3. Check that metadata.json files are valid JSON
4. Check that message bundle files parse correctly with Rust models
5. Report any incompatibilities found

This should be a test/verification step, not a conversion step -- the formats
are expected to be identical. If any differences are found, they become new tasks.

File: crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/compat.rs (NEW module)
Or: test in crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:19:15.841993181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:26:27.822904929Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:26:27.822886164Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:19:15.841993181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":909,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: Also verify that Python-written metadata.json files contain all fields the Rust models expect. If any optional Python fields are missing in Rust, the Rust deserializer must handle them gracefully (Option<T> or default). Check for edge cases: empty projects, projects with no messages, projects with only file reservations.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:10:51Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-legjy.3","title":"Track C: Fix frankentui event-loop drain + resize churn (/dp/frankentui)","description":"## Purpose
Fix event-loop and resize handling inefficiencies in `/dp/frankentui` that create busy-loop behavior and visible redraw churn.

## Scope
1. Audit event drain policy that repeatedly polls with zero timeout during bursts.
2. Implement bounded/fair event draining to avoid monopolizing CPU while keeping input responsiveness.
3. Debounce/coalesce resize (`SIGWINCH`) handling and redraw invalidation to prevent flashing storms.
4. Add PTY-focused regression tests for traversal responsiveness and visual stability.

## Critical Constraint
Preserve interactive feel and keyboard responsiveness; no blunt throttling that makes controls sluggish.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Zero-timeout event-drain spin is bounded and no longer dominates traces.
2. Resize storms are coalesced without visual corruption.
3. Input latency remains within budget under event pressure.
4. PTY regressions cover both normal and storm conditions.
Plan-space hardening requirements:
1. Comprehensive unit tests must cover happy path, edge cases, boundary conditions, invariants, and failure paths introduced by this bead.
2. Comprehensive integration/E2E (PTY/stress where relevant) scripts must validate end-user behavior, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead.
3. Detailed diagnostics logging/artifacts are mandatory: scenario IDs, timestamps, timing breakdowns (p50/p95/p99 where applicable), failure reason codes, key counters, and artifact file paths.
4. Verification commands and reproduction steps must be documented so failures are locally reproducible by another engineer.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:27.935918340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:42.966069517Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","incident","perf","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-legjy.3","depends_on_id":"br-legjy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:27.935918340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-legjy.3","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:51.778547584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":935,"issue_id":"br-legjy.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track addresses framework-level fairness. App-level fixes alone are insufficient if the event loop keeps spinning in zero-timeout drain bursts.","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-legjy.3.1","title":"C1: Profile event-drain hotpath and quantify poll(0) burst impact in /dp/frankentui","description":"## Intent
Quantify event-loop cost in `/dp/frankentui`, especially zero-timeout drain bursts.

## Scope
1. Instrument poll/drain loop behavior during traversal and bursty input.
2. Measure proportion of loops spent in immediate drain mode (`timeout=0`).
3. Correlate drain bursts with output write bursts and visible flashing reports.
4. Build a compact profile report that drives policy changes.

## Deliverable
Measured event-loop profile with clear optimization targets.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Profile captures poll/drain timing and mode distribution.
2. Zero-timeout burst behavior is quantified under reproducer.
3. Correlation to redraw/write churn is documented.
4. Findings produce concrete change requirements for C2/C3.
Plan-space hardening requirements:
1. Comprehensive unit tests must cover happy path, edge cases, boundary conditions, invariants, and failure paths introduced by this bead.
2. Comprehensive integration/E2E (PTY/stress where relevant) scripts must validate end-user behavior, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead.
3. Detailed diagnostics logging/artifacts are mandatory: scenario IDs, timestamps, timing breakdowns (p50/p95/p99 where applicable), failure reason codes, key counters, and artifact file paths.
4. Verification commands and reproduction steps must be documented so failures are locally reproducible by another engineer.","notes":"2026-02-27 IcyAnchor profile complete (C1): captured syscall-level evidence for zero-timeout poll behavior and burst drain impact in /data/projects/frankentui.

Repro commands (rch/offloaded for cargo work):
1) rch exec -- cargo test -p ftui-tty -- --list
2) rch exec -- bash -lc "cd /data/projects/frankentui && strace -ff -tt -T -e trace=poll,ppoll,read,write -o /tmp/br-legjy-3-1-valid/case1.strace cargo test -p ftui-tty tests::poll_returns_buffered_events_immediately -- --nocapture"
3) rch exec -- bash -lc "cd /data/projects/frankentui && strace -ff -tt -T -e trace=poll,ppoll,read,write -o /tmp/br-legjy-3-1-valid/case2.strace cargo test -p ftui-tty tests::pipe_large_ascii_burst_roundtrips -- --nocapture"
4) refined binary-level run using ftui_tty lib-test executable from --no-run JSON to reduce cargo noise; artifacts under /tmp/br-legjy-3-1-final.

Scenario IDs + metrics (binary-level artifacts):
- C1-case1 (tests::poll_returns_buffered_events_immediately): polls=4, poll(0)=2 (50.00%), reads=48, writes=20, top poll latency=22us.
- C1-case2 (tests::pipe_large_ascii_burst_roundtrips): polls=4, poll(0)=2 (50.00%), reads=1070, writes=20, top poll latencies=453us/362us.
- C1-case2 burst detail: 1024 reads at 8192 bytes each (~8.39 MiB) drained after readiness, showing high burst-drain throughput and concentrated syscall churn.

Representative evidence:
- poll(0) lines: poll([{fd=0,events=0},{fd=1,events=0},{fd=2,events=0}],3,0)=0 (Timeout)
- burst line: poll([{fd=3,events=POLLIN}],1,100)=1 followed by long 8192-byte read sequence.

Artifacts:
- /tmp/br-legjy-3-1-final/{no_run.jsonl,exe_path.txt,case1.*,case2.*,metrics.txt}
- /tmp/br-legjy-3-1-valid/{case1.rch.log,case2.rch.log,case1.strace.*,case2.strace.*}

C2/C3 change requirements derived:
1) Add bounded/fair immediate-drain budget around runtime poll(0) loop (iteration and/or time budget) to avoid zero-timeout spin storms under sustained queues.
2) Add adaptive fallback timeout/backoff when immediate-drain budget is exceeded.
3) Keep read burst handling but split diagnostics counters by source (poll(0) loops vs readiness poll>0) to isolate churn drivers.
4) Correlation note: input burst path is read-heavy with low write churn in these repros; visible flashing likely comes from render/present cadence, not parser writes directly.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IcyAnchor","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:42.137488061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:45:42.624623493Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T06:45:42.624598817Z","close_reason":"Completed C1 profiling with artifact-backed poll(0)/burst metrics and C2/C3 requirements","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","incident","perf","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-legjy.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:27.684056485Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-legjy.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:42.137488061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":947,"issue_id":"br-legjy.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Observed symptom to target: repeated poll(...,0) loops that monopolize cycles during bursts.","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:13Z"}]}

-{"id":"br-legjy.3.3","title":"C3: Implement SIGWINCH debounce/coalesced resize invalidation to stop redraw storms","description":"## Intent
Stop resize-signal storms from causing repeated full redraw churn and flashing.

## Scope
1. Debounce/coalesce resize events into stable invalidation windows.
2. Ensure only necessary layout recompute/render passes occur per effective size change.
3. Preserve correctness on real terminal resizes and rapid drag-resize scenarios.
4. Add diagnostics around resize-event compression and redraw count.

## Deliverable
Robust resize handling that avoids storm-driven flashing.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Resize storms no longer trigger redundant redraw loops.

3. Flashing artifacts from resize churn are eliminated in PTY replay.

4. Verification commands and reproduction steps must be documented so failures are locally reproducible by another engineer.","notes":"2026-02-27 IcyAnchor implementation progress:

Code changes (crates/ftui-runtime/src/program.rs):
- Added ImmediateDrainConfig (max_zero_timeout_polls_per_burst, max_burst_duration, backoff_timeout) with defaults 64 / 2ms / 1ms.
- Added ImmediateDrainStats counters (bursts, zero_timeout_polls, backoff_polls, capped_bursts, max_zero_timeout_polls_in_burst).
- Extended ProgramConfig with immediate_drain + builder with_immediate_drain().
- Added Program fields immediate_drain_config/immediate_drain_stats and accessor immediate_drain_stats().
- Replaced unbounded inline poll(0) drain loop with drain_ready_events() policy:
  * immediate draining via poll_event(Duration::ZERO)
  * cap on consecutive zero-timeout polls and burst wall-time
  * fairness backoff path: std::thread::yield_now() + non-zero poll(backoff_timeout)
  * per-burst stats tracking.

New tests:
- program_config_with_immediate_drain
- immediate_drain_budget_adds_backoff_poll_under_burst
- immediate_drain_zero_poll_limit_is_clamped

Verification (rch, /data/projects/frankentui):

2) rch exec -- cargo test -p ftui-runtime immediate_drain_budget_adds_backoff_poll_under_burst -- --nocapture  (pass)

+{"id":"br-legjy.3.3","title":"C3: Implement SIGWINCH debounce/coalesced resize invalidation to stop redraw storms","description":"## Intent
Stop resize-signal storms from causing repeated full redraw churn and flashing.

## Scope
1. Debounce/coalesce resize events into stable invalidation windows.
2. Ensure only necessary layout recompute/render passes occur per effective size change.
3. Preserve correctness on real terminal resizes and rapid drag-resize scenarios.
4. Add diagnostics around resize-event compression and redraw count.

## Deliverable
Robust resize handling that avoids storm-driven flashing.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Resize storms no longer trigger redundant redraw loops.
2. Final layout correctness is preserved after bursty resizes.
3. Flashing artifacts from resize churn are eliminated in PTY replay.
4. Diagnostics show effective event coalescing.

 {"id":"br-legjy.4","title":"Track D: mcp-agent-mail TUI hotpath corrections (scheduler, invalidation, overdraw)","description":"## Purpose
Remove app-layer hotpath mistakes in `mcp-agent-mail-server` that perform expensive work for inactive screens and over-render unchanged regions.

## Scope
1. Replace all-screen tick behavior with active-screen-centric scheduling plus explicit low-rate background maintenance.
2. Introduce dirty/invalidation semantics so unchanged screen state skips heavy recompute/render paths.
3. Stratify poller/data-refresh cadence by visibility and urgency.
4. Remove redundant clears/overdraw and unnecessary tiny write bursts where possible.
5. Ensure event queue/backpressure handling stays robust during bursts.

## Critical Constraint
No visual redesign; retain current UX output while drastically reducing wasted work.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Inactive screens no longer execute heavyweight tick/update loops at active cadence.
2. Dirty-state logic demonstrably reduces recompute and redraw volume.
3. Poller cadence respects data freshness needs without generating hot idle churn.
4. Rendering churn reduction does not alter screen appearance semantics.
Plan-space hardening requirements:
1. Comprehensive unit tests must cover happy path, edge cases, boundary conditions, invariants, and failure paths introduced by this bead.

 {"id":"br-legjy.4.1","title":"D1: Replace all-screen tick loop with visibility-aware scheduler in tui_app","description":"## Intent
Eliminate wasted per-tick work by removing all-screen active cadence in `tui_app`.

## Scope
1. Replace global all-screen tick dispatch with active-screen-first scheduling.
2. Introduce low-rate background maintenance for inactive screens only where needed.
3. Preserve behavior for time-sensitive features (toasts, overlays, alerts).
4. Add instrumentation proving reduced inactive work.

## Deliverable
Visibility-aware scheduler in app layer with behavior parity.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Inactive screens are no longer ticked at active cadence by default.

4. Instrumentation shows reduced tick workload and CPU.
